rationale: Packaging refactor that moves three subsystems (stores, embeddings, rerankers) into a new sibling package. User has decided the strategy: Option B (PEP 420 namespace package) — the new dist contributes modules under parrot.* so existing imports stay byte-identical. The plan now focuses on (a) verifying PEP 420 feasibility on the host (parrot/__init__.py shape, host pyproject package-discovery config), (b) inventorying the three subsystems' base-vs-concrete split, (c) enumerating extras that move, (d) mapping Registry resolution strategy per subsystem, and (e) finding every coupling site that has to keep working under the new namespace.

queries.intent: Read the host parrot package __init__.py to determine whether it can act as a PEP 420 namespace package (empty/missing) or whether it has side effects that block satellite distributions from contributing submodules.

Map the existing sibling-package top-levels (loaders/tools/pipelines). User has explicitly waived the precedent for ai-parrot-embeddings, but the tree is still useful to verify they don't accidentally shadow parrot/* names that would conflict with the new namespace-package layout.
Enumerate the parrot.embeddings module tree to map base/abstract/registry vs concrete backends.
Enumerate the parrot.stores module tree (the vector-store subsystem).
Enumerate the parrot.rerankers module tree.
Read the ai-parrot pyproject in full — enumerate every extra and meta-extra tied to stores/embeddings/rerankers and every dependency block that will move with them.
Read the embeddings public surface (__init__.py) — dictates what callers see.
Read the embeddings Registry — verify whether resolution is direct import, string import, or entry-point based.
Read the embeddings Abstract base — the contract that stays in core.
Read the embeddings catalog — extras-key to concrete-class map.
Read the parrot.stores public surface (__init__.py).
Read the parrot.rerankers public surface (__init__.py).
Inspect ai-parrot-loaders pyproject — the canonical sibling-package precedent (deps, optional-deps, ai-parrot core dep).
Inspect ai-parrot-tools pyproject — second precedent, likely the one with per-backend extras (closest analog).
Inspect ai-parrot-pipelines pyproject — third precedent, useful for entry-point patterns.

Find every consumer of the three subsystems inside ai-parrot core (handlers, RAG pipelines, agents, tools) — sizes downstream impact and identifies imports that must keep working under either Option A or Option B.
Identify the coupling sites pre-flagged in the source: handlers/stores, tools/vectorstoresearch, tools/multistoresearch, plus any others tying ai-parrot core to stores/embeddings/rerankers.

Locate the concrete backends mentioned by the user (pgvector, milvus, arangodb, bigquery, faiss) — confirm they live in parrot/stores and not embeddings, and whether their deps are isolated behind extras today.
Find entry-point definitions in any sibling pyproject — the cleanest mechanism for a satellite package to register classes back into a Registry that lives in the host package.

Check namespace/package configuration in the host ai-parrot pyproject (find_packages vs find_namespace_packages, [tool.setuptools.packages.find], where = src). Decides whether Option B works as-is or whether the host config must be amended before the new package can contribute submodules.
Find recent activity on the three subsystem trees (embeddings, stores, rerankers) to avoid colliding with an in-flight refactor.
Recent activity on parrot/stores.
Recent activity on parrot/rerankers.
Recent activity on packages/ as a whole — captures the latest sibling-package decisions / conventions.
Search prior SDD work for any existing decision on embeddings/stores/packaging splits or namespace-package strategy.
Look for the agents-lite profile and embeddings meta-extra definitions in the host pyproject — they'll need migration aliases.
Confirm whether parrot/bots/stores and parrot/handlers/stores are actually vector-store related (and thus subject to move) or unrelated state stores (stay in core).
Check top-level docs (CONTEXT.md, README, roadmap) for stated direction on the embeddings/packaging split — avoid contradicting documented plans.

expected_findings: Verification that Option B (PEP 420 namespace package) is technically feasible: parrot/__init__.py is absent or namespace-compatible, and the host pyproject uses find_namespace_packages (or equivalent) for discovery.
Inventory of every extra tied to stores/embeddings/rerankers in the host pyproject, classified as moves vs stays.
Resolution mechanism for each of the three Registries (direct, string, entry points) — drives the registration strategy of the new package.
Concrete file list per subsystem (embeddings/stores/rerankers): which files are base/Registry/Abstract (stay) vs concrete backend (move).
Downstream import sites in ai-parrot core that must keep working byte-identically under the new namespace.
Recent commit signals confirming no in-flight refactor collision on any of the three subsystems.
